definition: prediction task that assigns an input to one of a set of discrete categories or classes.
unsupervised method that groups unlabeled observations into clusters based on similarity, proximity, or shared patterns.
prediction task that estimates a numeric, continuous output value such as price, temperature, duration, or quantity.
density-based clustering algorithm that forms clusters from closely packed points and labels isolated low-density points as outliers or noise.
set of techniques that reduce the number of predictor variables while preserving essential structure or information in the data.
ensemble learning technique that builds models sequentially, with each new learner trained to correct errors made by the previous ones.
unsupervised clustering algorithm that organises data into a tree of nested clusters, commonly through agglomerative or divisive merging and splitting.
unsupervised clustering algorithm that partitions unlabeled data into K clusters by iteratively assigning points to cluster centroids and updating those centroids.
regularized linear regression method that uses an L1 penalty to shrink coefficients and can reduce some coefficients to zero, simplifying the model by removing less useful variables.
topic-modeling method that represents documents using latent topics, where each topic is characterized by keywords rather than explicit names or descriptions.
regression method that models a dependent variable as a weighted combination of one or more input variables, producing a best-fit linear relationship for prediction.
classification model that maps inputs to probabilities, often with an S-shaped logistic function, and uses those probabilities to assign categories such as yes/no outcomes.
feature-extraction and matrix-factorization method that decomposes a nonnegative data matrix into lower-rank nonnegative factors to uncover meaningful patterns, topics, or themes.
dimensionality reduction technique that transforms correlated variables into a smaller set of uncorrelated principal components that retain most of the original information.
ensemble learning algorithm that combines the predictions of many decision trees to produce a single result for classification or regression.
regularized linear regression method that shrinks coefficient magnitudes with an L2 penalty, reducing the influence of predictors without typically driving coefficients exactly to zero.
machine learning approach that trains a model on labeled data so it learns patterns linking inputs to known outputs and can predict outputs for new data.
nonlinear dimensionality reduction algorithm used mainly for visualization in 2D or 3D, aiming to keep nearby points in high-dimensional space close together in the lower-dimensional map.

Nonlinear dimensionality reduction technique used for visualisation and general dimension reduction, mapping high-dimensional data into a lower-dimensional space while preserving important local structure.
machine learning approach that trains on unlabeled data to discover intrinsic patterns, structure, dependencies, or correlations without external ground-truth labels.
